title: Face Reading — The Window to Character and Destiny
summary: Chinese face reading (Mian Xiang) analyzes facial features to reveal personality, health, and life patterns.
content:
Face reading (面相) is an ancient Chinese practice based on the principle that the face reflects the mind. It is not about predicting an unchangeable fate, but about understanding patterns.

【The Twelve Palaces】
The face is divided into twelve sections, each corresponding to an area of life:
- The Life Palace (between brows): Overall vitality
- The Wealth Palace (nose tip): Financial prospects
- The Sibling Palace (eyebrows): Relationships with siblings
- The Spouse Palace (eye corners): Marriage
- The Children Palace (below eyes): Offspring
- The Health Palace (nose bridge): Physical wellbeing
- The Travel Palace (temples): Adventure and mobility
- The Career Palace (forehead): Professional life

【The Five Organs of the Face】
Ears: Childhood luck and kidney energy.
Eyebrows: Relationships and longevity.
Eyes: Wisdom, insight, and the spirit.
Nose: Wealth and middle-age fortune.
Mouth: Expression and later-life fortune.

Face reading offers a framework for self-understanding, not a fixed verdict.

title: Palm Reading — The Universe in Your Hands
summary: Chinese palm reading examines the lines, shapes, and mounds of the hand to reveal character and destiny.
content:
Palm reading (手相) is a traditional art that finds the map of a person's life in the lines and contours of their hands.

【The Three Main Lines】
1. Life Line: Curves around the thumb. Represents vitality and physical health.
2. Head Line: Crosses the palm horizontally. Represents intellect and thinking style.
3. Heart Line: Above the head line. Represents emotions and relationships.

【Minor Lines】
- Marriage Line: Small lines above the heart line under the little finger.
- Fate Line: Runs from the wrist toward the middle finger. Shows life path.
- Sun Line: Below the ring finger. Associated with fame and creativity.

【The Mounts】
The fleshy pads of the palm are called mounts, each associated with different qualities: Jupiter mount (ambition), Saturn mount (wisdom), Sun mount (creativity), Mercury mount (communication).

title: Detailed Explanation of the Five Facial Features
summary: From the eyes to the eyebrows, from the nose to the mouth, every facial feature hides the code of personality.
content:
In physiognomy, the 'Five Features'—ears, eyebrows, eyes, nose, and mouth—correspond to different aspects of life.

【Eyes—The Window to the Soul】
The eyes are the most important part of physiognomy. People with large eyes are emotionally rich; those with small eyes are meticulous. Bright-eyed individuals are energetic; those with upward-slanting outer corners have leadership qualities.

【Nose—The Palace of Wealth】
The nose represents wealth and mid-life fortune. A straight nose bridge indicates strong opinions; a round nose tip suggests good financial luck. Full nostrils indicate strong wealth accumulation ability; exposed nostrils may lead to financial loss.

【Mouth—The Official of Income and Expenditure】
It represents blessings and late-life fortune. Rosy lips indicate good fortune; upward-turned mouth corners suggest optimism and cheerfulness.

【Eyebrows—The Official of Longevity】
They represent interpersonal relationships. Thick eyebrows indicate a straightforward personality; a wide space between eyebrows suggests an open-minded nature.

Physiognomy should be considered holistically; harmony and balance among the five features constitute a good face.
